selected_path.rasterization: managed CPU density and GMI rasterization outside per-event WPF objects
selected_path.composition: WPF compositor; functional correctness does not depend on GPU availability
selected_path.fallback: retain the same BGRA32 and WritePixels seam when WPF selects software composition
selected_path.maintenance_boundary: Core owns deterministic raster buffers; the WPF adapter owns only dispatcher scheduling and bitmap copy
selected_path.phase2_boundary: port the Core contracts and thin WPF adapter; do not port prototype observations or promote them to throughput guarantees

candidates.correctness: deterministic density and GMI raster hashes plus owner-thread bitmap copy pass
candidates.performance: official AB-PERF-REF-v1 hard scatter and combined runs pass independently 3/3 each; the ten-minute soak passes; headroom remains a separate observation
candidates.dependency_license: built-in WPF; zero external NuGet; installed .NET MIT and third-party notice hashes pinned
candidates.cpu_gpu_behavior: CPU binning/rasterization and CPU-to-bitmap copy; WPF controls final composition
candidates.fallback: same bounded BGRA32 WritePixels seam under framework-selected software composition
candidates.maintenance: small managed adapter with no unsafe pointer lifetime or third-party renderer lifecycle
candidates.phase2_portability: directly portable as the Core raster contract plus a thin WPF adapter; prototype observations remain excluded

candidates.correctness: would require explicit Lock/Unlock, dirty-rectangle, stride, and pointer-lifetime proofs not needed by the accepted copy seam
candidates.performance: no measured need to accept unsafe lifetime complexity before the reference-PC checkpoint
candidates.cpu_gpu_behavior: CPU writes through a mutable back buffer; WPF controls final composition
candidates.phase2_portability: would require new unsafe lifetime and recovery contracts before Phase 2 adoption

candidates.correctness: per-event WPF objects conflict with the density-raster and bounded-history contract
candidates.performance: not admitted to performance comparison because it violates the frozen seam
candidates.cpu_gpu_behavior: framework-retained visual objects and composition obscure the fixed raster workload
candidates.maintenance: object-lifecycle and retained-visual complexity scale with displayed events
candidates.phase2_portability: not portable to Phase 2 because it violates the bounded raster and no-per-event-object seam

stop_conditions: Any external renderer or rendering/test/benchmark package requires a task_management amendment before download or implementation.
Any .NET SDK, Desktop Runtime, target framework, Release x64, or offline restore pin change stops this decision.
A miss on AB-PERF-REF-v1 cannot be bypassed by this compatible-PC development evidence or by the headroom observation.
residual_limits: Official acceptance is limited to the frozen P0-R1 fixtures, AB-PERF-REF-v1, AB-PERF-RUNNER-v1, and the recorded source revision.
The headroom result remains observation-only and does not substitute for a hard scatter, combined, or soak gate.
This decision selects the Phase 2 rendering seam and does not certify a production shell, acquisition isolation, or production throughput.
